recap: Mean = sum / count. Median = middle value in ordered list. Mode = most frequent. Range = max - min. For grouped data use midpoints to estimate the mean.

steps: 7 appears 3 times — more than any other value.

Q2.question: Find the median and range of: 12, 5, 19, 8, 14.

Q3.question: The frequency table shows scores: Score 2 (freq 3), Score 4 (freq 5), Score 6 (freq 2), Score 8 (freq 4), Score 10 (freq 1). Calculate the mean.

Q4.question: The grouped frequency table shows heights: 140-149 (freq 4), 150-159 (freq 11), 160-169 (freq 9), 170-179 (freq 6). Estimate the mean height.

Q5.question: Class A has a mean score of 64 and 20 students. Class B has a mean of 71 and 15 students. Find the combined mean of all 35 students.

summary: Mean = total sum / number of values; for frequency tables use sum of (value x frequency) / total frequency.
Always sort data before finding the median; for an even number of values average the two middle ones.
For grouped data, use class midpoints to estimate the mean.
When comparing two distributions, comment on both an average and the spread.
